name: Brazil Nut
summary: The single richest food source of selenium — so concentrated that one or two nuts can cover a day's requirement.
superfoodReason: By far the most selenium-dense food known; a single nut can supply an entire day's selenium.

benefits.mechanism: Selenium is built into antioxidant enzymes (glutathione peroxidases) and is required to produce active thyroid hormone. Brazil nuts concentrate it like no other food — which is also why you shouldn't eat many, as excess selenium is toxic.

citations.title: Selenium — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: Selenium content varies enormously with the soil the tree grew in — a single Brazil nut can range from about 70 to more than 90 micrograms, meaning one nut can exceed the daily requirement. Two to three a day is plenty; more risks toxicity.

cautions.effect: The classic signs of too much selenium are a garlicky or metallic taste and garlic-smelling breath, then brittle hair and nails — and hair loss if you keep going. Nausea and a rash can come with it. It reverses when you cut back.
cautions.threshold: A single nut can carry 70–90+ µg, so a small handful daily can pass the 400 µg adult upper limit. Two or three a day is plenty.

citations.title: Selenium — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)
